title: Tests 4
description: The `#[should_panic]` attribute marks a test that is expected to panic. The test passes if the code panics.
instructions: Add the should_panic attribute to the test that expects a panic.
hints: Use `#[should_panic]` to mark a test that should panic.
You can add an expected message: #[should_panic(expected = "...")]
Place the attribute right above the test function.
explanation: The `#[should_panic]` attribute indicates that a test is expected to panic. If the function doesn't panic, the test fails. You can optionally specify `expected = "substring"` to check that the panic message contains a specific string. This is useful for testing error conditions and input validation.
